executive_summary: Repoints the three remaining fleet inbox readers -- Adam advisory inbox (scripts/adam-advisory.cjs), Solomon drain (scripts/solomon-advisory.cjs), and the coordinator tick lanes (scripts/coordinator-quiet-tick.mjs + lib/coordinator/adam-advisory-store.cjs) -- onto Child A's fail-open registry-reader lib (lib/fleet/drain-set-registry.js) instead of each maintaining its own hand-rolled recognized-kind list. Research found the Adam site's ADAM_INBOX_KINDS constant has drifted 8 kinds ahead of the hard-coded DRAIN_SETS.adam fallback (chairman_heads_up, chairman_handoff, coordinator_advisory, coordinator_adam_feedback, assist_request, reconcile_consult, coordinator_source_request, coordinator_review) -- a naive repoint would silently narrow Adam's drain lane and regress those 8 kinds into the orphan path, so this SD first reconciles DRAIN_SETS.adam to include them (byte-identical-behavior requirement, same discipline as Child A's own R2 reconciliation) before repointing. The coordinator tick lane's repoint directly closes the class of bug behind the 2026-07-19 lane-blindness incident (commit bb661ec627e / QF-20260719-298): a single hard-coded adam_advisory kind-check was patched in ad-hoc after 14 advisory rows sat unactioned because the tick's own signal_type-only salience state was structurally blind to payload.kind rows; this SD generalizes that check through the registry-reader so any future kind added to the coordinator's drain set gets tick representation automatically, not just adam_advisory. A grep-style static guard (modeled on tests/static-guards/cleanup-pending-pairing.test.js's PINNED_READERS pattern) asserts zero residual hand-rolled per-role kind-list constants remain outside the sanctioned lib/fleet/worker-status.cjs DRAIN_SETS fallback + the registry-reader lib itself.

functional_requirements.title: Reconcile DRAIN_SETS.adam with ADAM_INBOX_KINDS (close the pre-existing drift before repointing)
functional_requirements.description: In lib/fleet/worker-status.cjs's DRAIN_SETS.adam array, add the 8 kinds present in scripts/adam-advisory.cjs's ADAM_INBOX_KINDS (lines ~435-469) but absent from DRAIN_SETS.adam: chairman_heads_up, chairman_handoff, coordinator_advisory, coordinator_adam_feedback, assist_request, reconcile_consult, coordinator_source_request, coordinator_review. This MUST land before FR-2's repoint -- otherwise resolveRecognizedKinds({role:'adam'}) (which falls open to DRAIN_SETS.adam) would return a narrower set than ADAM_INBOX_KINDS ever did, silently regressing those 8 kinds out of Adam's drain lane into the orphan/WARN-only path. None of these 8 kinds exist yet as PAYLOAD_KINDS constants (grep confirmed) -- add them as new PAYLOAD_KINDS entries with a provenance comment citing this reconciliation, then reference them in DRAIN_SETS.adam, matching the existing style of every other DRAIN_SETS entry (constant reference, not a raw string literal).
acceptance_criteria: DRAIN_SETS.adam contains all 8 previously-missing kinds, referenced via new PAYLOAD_KINDS constants (not raw strings), each with a provenance comment citing SD-LEO-INFRA-DRAIN-SET-REGISTRY-001-C
A test asserts DRAIN_SETS.adam is now a superset of (byte-identical in content to, modulo array order) ADAM_INBOX_KINDS before FR-2 removes the latter
No other role's DRAIN_SETS entry is modified by this FR

functional_requirements.title: Repoint Adam advisory inbox (scripts/adam-advisory.cjs) onto the registry-reader, subtracting ADAM_EXCLUDED_KINDS
functional_requirements.description: Replace isAdamInboxRow's use of the local ADAM_INBOX_KINDS constant (scripts/adam-advisory.cjs:435-479) with a resolved-kinds array sourced from lib/fleet/drain-set-registry.js's resolveRecognizedKinds({supabase, role:'adam'}), called once per drainInbox invocation (drainInbox is already async, :590) and threaded into the (still-synchronous) isAdamInboxRow/isOrphanedAdamRow predicates as a parameter rather than a closed-over module constant. CRITICAL DESIGN REQUIREMENT (found during PLAN-phase TESTING sub-agent review): DRAIN_SETS.adam is a strict SUPERSET of ADAM_INBOX_KINDS -- it also includes canary_request, comms_check, and cross_party_ping, all three of which are in worker-status.cjs's ADAM_EXCLUDED_KINDS (:144) precisely because they are handler-owned, mechanical, or otherwise NOT meant to be consumed by the generic inbox drain. A naive repoint that feeds the raw resolved-kinds array straight into isAdamInboxRow would consume these handler-owned rows before their dedicated responders act on them -- the exact last-hop silent-death class this SD family exists to prevent. The repoint MUST subtract ADAM_EXCLUDED_KINDS (already exported from worker-status.cjs) from the resolved-kinds array before passing it to isAdamInboxRow/isOrphanedAdamRow. Remove the ADAM_INBOX_KINDS local constant once FR-1's reconciliation + this subtraction make the registry-reader's (excluded-kinds-subtracted) resolved set byte-identical to it.
acceptance_criteria: scripts/adam-advisory.cjs no longer defines or references a local ADAM_INBOX_KINDS array
isAdamInboxRow receives its recognized-kinds set as a parameter (resolved once per drainInbox call via resolveRecognizedKinds, with ADAM_EXCLUDED_KINDS subtracted), not a module-level constant

A test drives isAdamInboxRow/isOrphanedAdamRow with the resolved-and-subtracted kinds array and confirms every kind formerly in ADAM_INBOX_KINDS (including the 8 reconciled in FR-1) still classifies as an inbox row, not orphaned

A NEGATIVE test confirms canary_request, comms_check, and cross_party_ping -- all present in DRAIN_SETS.adam but excluded via ADAM_EXCLUDED_KINDS -- still classify as NON-inbox (not consumed by the generic drain) after the repoint, proving the subtraction actually happens and is not a no-op
supabase=null / registry-query-error paths fail open to the same reconciled-and-subtracted DRAIN_SETS.adam list (via the registry-reader's own fail-open contract) -- no new failure mode introduced

functional_requirements.title: Repoint Solomon drain (scripts/solomon-advisory.cjs) onto the registry-reader, subtracting comms_check
functional_requirements.description: Replace isSolomonInboxRow's use of the local SOLOMON_INBOX_KINDS constant (scripts/solomon-advisory.cjs:165-169) with a resolved-kinds array from resolveRecognizedKinds({supabase, role:'solomon'}), called once per drainInbox invocation (:278) and threaded into the synchronous predicate as a parameter. CRITICAL DESIGN REQUIREMENT (found during PLAN-phase TESTING sub-agent review): DRAIN_SETS.solomon ALREADY contains comms_check (worker-status.cjs:161) -- unlike SOLOMON_INBOX_KINDS, which deliberately omits it because comms_check is handled by drainInbox's own separate first-class branch (:299-313), evaluated BEFORE the generic isSolomonInboxRow check. A naive repoint that feeds the raw resolved-kinds array into isSolomonInboxRow would fold comms_check into the generic filter and break that first-class branch's exclusivity. The repoint MUST subtract {comms_check} from the resolved-kinds array before passing it to isSolomonInboxRow, so the generic filter's behavior for comms_check is unchanged (still handled exclusively by the dedicated branch). Remove the SOLOMON_INBOX_KINDS local constant once repointed.
acceptance_criteria: scripts/solomon-advisory.cjs no longer defines or references a local SOLOMON_INBOX_KINDS array
isSolomonInboxRow receives its recognized-kinds set as a parameter resolved via resolveRecognizedKinds with comms_check subtracted, not a module-level constant

The separate comms_check first-class branch (:299-313) is untouched -- a test confirms comms_check routing behavior (which branch handles it, and that it is NOT double-counted or reclassified by the generic filter) is byte-identical before and after this repoint
A NEGATIVE test confirms isSolomonInboxRow(comms_check-kind row) returns false post-repoint even though comms_check is present in DRAIN_SETS.solomon, proving the subtraction actually happens
A test confirms every kind formerly in SOLOMON_INBOX_KINDS still classifies as an inbox row post-repoint

functional_requirements.title: Close the coordinator tick's payload.kind blindness by generalizing readSalientState's salience check (NOT selectUnactionedAdvisories's retirement logic)
functional_requirements.description: The 2026-07-19 lane-blindness incident (commit bb661ec627e, QF-20260719-298): scripts/coordinator-quiet-tick.mjs's readSalientState's openSignalCount (lines ~140-148) counts rows via payload->>signal_type IS NOT NULL only, structurally blind to payload.kind-only rows -- 14 adam_advisory rows sat unactioned with zero tick representation until an ad-hoc single-kind (ADAM_ADVISORY_KIND='adam_advisory', lib/coordinator/adam-advisory-store.cjs:18) patch was applied as a targeted stopgap (a separate count + forced ping, not a generalization of the salience check itself). SCOPE CORRECTION (found during PLAN-phase TESTING sub-agent review): generalizing selectUnactionedAdvisories's kind filter directly -- as originally scoped -- is UNSAFE, because that function couples its kind filter to an actioned_at-null retirement predicate specific to adam_advisory's retirement semantics; other kinds in the coordinator's/adam's resolved set are retired via read_at/acknowledged_at or never retired via actioned_at at all (handler-owned/mechanical kinds), so a naive kind-filter generalization there would create a PERMANENT PHANTOM COUNT for those kinds -- worse than the original blindness, and self-inflicted rather than incidental. Instead, this FR generalizes readSalientState's openSignalCount ONLY: OR in an additional salience term -- payload->>kind IN (resolveRecognizedKinds({role:'coordinator'}) minus any handler-owned/mechanical exclusions) -- alongside the existing payload->>signal_type IS NOT NULL term, so ANY future kind added to the coordinator's drain set contributes tick salience/delta detection automatically. selectUnactionedAdvisories's existing single-kind + actioned_at-coupled retirement logic is explicitly LEFT UNCHANGED and OUT OF SCOPE for this SD -- generalizing per-kind retirement-predicate semantics correctly is a larger, separate piece of work, noted as a risk/follow-on below. hasUnactionedDirective (:178-193, using imported DIRECTIVE_KINDS) is a SEPARATE, already-correct lane and is NOT in scope for this FR -- do not touch it. All touched functions in this site are already async end-to-end, so no new async plumbing is required.
acceptance_criteria: readSalientState's openSignalCount now counts a row if EITHER payload.signal_type IS NOT NULL OR payload.kind is in the coordinator's resolved recognized-kinds set (minus handler-owned/mechanical exclusions) -- an OR-in of a new salience term, not a replacement of the existing one

selectUnactionedAdvisories (lib/coordinator/adam-advisory-store.cjs) and its actioned_at-coupled retirement logic are UNMODIFIED by this FR -- a test/diff check confirms this file has zero functional changes

A test injects a NEW kind (not adam_advisory) into a mocked resolveRecognizedKinds response and confirms openSignalCount now reflects it -- proving the salience-check generalization actually works, not just re-verifying the existing adam_advisory case
A test confirms a kind NOT in the resolved set (or explicitly excluded as handler-owned) does NOT inflate openSignalCount, so the OR-in cannot itself create a new phantom-count class
hasUnactionedDirective and its DIRECTIVE_KINDS usage are unmodified by this FR
Existing behavior for adam_advisory rows (the incident's own case) is unchanged end-to-end: same openSignalCount contribution, same selectUnactionedAdvisories count, same forced-ping trigger

functional_requirements.title: Grep-style static guard: zero residual hand-rolled per-role drain-kind constants
functional_requirements.description: Create tests/static-guards/drain-set-registry-readers.test.js modeled on tests/static-guards/cleanup-pending-pairing.test.js's PINNED_READERS pattern: pin lib/fleet/drain-set-registry.js as the sole legitimate resolver of 'recognized kinds for role X', allowlist lib/fleet/worker-status.cjs (DRAIN_SETS + ADAM_EXCLUDED_KINDS, the sanctioned SSOT + exclusion list, must remain), and FAIL if any file outside that allowlist defines a new array shaped like a per-role recognized-kind list. DETECTION IS SEMANTIC/CONTENT-SHAPE, NOT IDENTIFIER-NAME: flag a frozen/const array of 3+ string literals matching known PAYLOAD_KINDS-style tokens (e.g. drawn from the DIRECTIVE_KINDS/PAYLOAD_KINDS vocabulary) assigned outside the allowlist -- an identifier-name regex like /_?INBOX_KINDS$/i is explicitly insufficient (trivially evaded by renaming) and must not be the sole detection mechanism. This directly asserts ADAM_INBOX_KINDS and SOLOMON_INBOX_KINDS (removed by FR-2/FR-3) do not reappear under any name, and that no NEW hand-rolled equivalent is introduced elsewhere.
acceptance_criteria: The guard fails if ADAM_INBOX_KINDS or SOLOMON_INBOX_KINDS (or an equivalently-shaped new constant) exists anywhere outside the allowlist
The guard passes DRAIN_SETS in lib/fleet/worker-status.cjs and lib/fleet/drain-set-registry.js itself (the sanctioned pair) without flagging them
The guard is added to the existing tests/static-guards/ suite and runs as part of the standard unit test pass (no new npm script needed)

technical_requirements.title: Zero behavior change while role_drain_sets remains unapplied
technical_requirements.description: Every repointed reader must classify the exact same rows as inbox/orphaned/unactioned as it did before this SD, verified against the reconciled DRAIN_SETS constant (fail-open fallback), WITH excluded/handler-owned kinds subtracted per FR-2/FR-3.

technical_requirements.title: No new async plumbing beyond what resolveRecognizedKinds requires
technical_requirements.description: All 3 reader sites already have an async caller in their call chain (drainInbox x2, the coordinator-quiet-tick main loop) -- resolve kinds once per invocation, thread as a parameter into synchronous predicates, do not make the predicates themselves async.

technical_requirements.title: comms_check's separate Solomon branch is untouched
technical_requirements.description: FR-3 must not fold comms_check handling into the resolved-kinds check; its existing first-class branch stays exactly as-is; comms_check is explicitly subtracted from Solomon's resolved-kinds array before the generic filter runs.

technical_requirements.title: Excluded/handler-owned kinds must be subtracted before generic-inbox consumption
technical_requirements.description: Both FR-2 (ADAM_EXCLUDED_KINDS) and FR-3 (comms_check) must subtract handler-owned kinds from the resolved-kinds array before it reaches the generic isXInboxRow predicate -- a raw pass-through of resolveRecognizedKinds() output is NOT correct for either site.

technical_requirements.title: selectUnactionedAdvisories's retirement-predicate coupling is out of scope
technical_requirements.description: FR-4 must not touch lib/coordinator/adam-advisory-store.cjs's selectUnactionedAdvisories or its actioned_at-coupled kind filter -- generalizing per-kind retirement semantics correctly is a larger follow-on, not this SD's job. FR-4 only OR's a new salience term into readSalientState's openSignalCount.

test_scenarios.expected: A test enumerates ADAM_INBOX_KINDS and asserts every kind is present in DRAIN_SETS.adam post-reconciliation, catching any future re-drift. A separate snapshot-style assertion confirms only DRAIN_SETS.adam changed (by exactly +8) and solomon/coordinator/worker arrays are byte-identical to their pre-FR-1 content -- protecting dispatch.cjs's (Child A) shared-constant WARN check from unintended blast radius.

test_scenarios.scenario: Adam inbox repoint behavior parity + exclusion proof (FR-2)
test_scenarios.expected: POSITIVE: for every kind formerly in ADAM_INBOX_KINDS (all 17, including the 8 reconciled), isAdamInboxRow classifies a row of that kind as inbox (not orphaned) when driven with the resolved-and-subtracted kinds array. NEGATIVE: for canary_request, comms_check, and cross_party_ping (all in DRAIN_SETS.adam, all in ADAM_EXCLUDED_KINDS), isAdamInboxRow/isOrphanedAdamRow do NOT classify them as generic-inbox rows post-repoint -- this is the assertion that would fail on a naive (non-subtracting) repoint.

test_scenarios.scenario: Solomon inbox repoint behavior parity + comms_check exclusion proof (FR-3)
test_scenarios.expected: POSITIVE: for every kind formerly in SOLOMON_INBOX_KINDS, isSolomonInboxRow classifies correctly with the resolved-and-subtracted kinds array. NEGATIVE: isSolomonInboxRow(comms_check-kind row) returns false post-repoint even though comms_check IS present in DRAIN_SETS.solomon -- this is the assertion that would fail on a naive (non-subtracting) repoint. A separate assertion confirms the dedicated comms_check branch (:299-313) still exclusively handles those rows (read_at/acknowledged_at or equivalent still stamped the same way as before this SD).

A test injects a NEW kind (not adam_advisory) into a mocked resolveRecognizedKinds({role:'coordinator'}) response and confirms openSignalCount now reflects a row of that kind -- proving the OR-in salience generalization actually works, not just re-verifying the existing adam_advisory case.

test_scenarios.scenario: FR-4 does not create a new phantom-count / regresses selectUnactionedAdvisories unmodified
test_scenarios.expected: A diff/content check confirms lib/coordinator/adam-advisory-store.cjs's selectUnactionedAdvisories function body is byte-identical before and after this SD (TR-5). A test confirms a resolved kind explicitly excluded as handler-owned/mechanical does NOT inflate openSignalCount. The exact adam_advisory count/forced-ping behavior from the original incident fix (commit bb661ec627e) still fires identically post-generalization.

test_scenarios.scenario: Static guard: zero residual hand-rolled kind-list constants, semantic-shape detection (FR-5)
test_scenarios.expected: tests/static-guards/drain-set-registry-readers.test.js fails if ADAM_INBOX_KINDS/SOLOMON_INBOX_KINDS or an equivalently-shaped new constant (detected by content/shape, e.g. a frozen array of >=3 string literals matching known PAYLOAD_KINDS-style tokens assigned outside the allowlist -- not merely by identifier-name regex, which a rename could evade) exists outside the allowlist; passes on the current post-repoint tree. Allowlist = lib/fleet/worker-status.cjs (DRAIN_SETS/ADAM_EXCLUDED_KINDS, the sanctioned SSOT + exclusion list) and lib/fleet/drain-set-registry.js.
Existing tests covering hasUnactionedDirective continue to pass unmodified, confirming FR-4 did not touch that separate lane.

acceptance_criteria: DRAIN_SETS.adam reconciled to include all 8 previously-drifted ADAM_INBOX_KINDS entries before any repoint lands
All 3 reader sites (Adam inbox, Solomon drain, coordinator tick lane) resolve recognized kinds through lib/fleet/drain-set-registry.js instead of a local hand-rolled constant
The coordinator tick lane's kind-check generalizes beyond the single adam_advisory hard-code, provably (TS-4) without regressing the existing case (TS-5)
A static guard enforces zero residual hand-rolled per-role kind-list constants outside the sanctioned DRAIN_SETS fallback
Zero behavior change to any of the 3 readers while role_drain_sets remains unapplied (Child A's migration is still STAGED/unapplied throughout this SD)

risks.risk: Reconciling DRAIN_SETS.adam (FR-1) is itself a behavior-affecting code change to a shared hard-coded constant consumed by dispatch.cjs's WARN check (Child A) -- a mistake here has fleet-wide blast radius, not just Adam's inbox.
risks.mitigation: TS-1 pins the reconciliation with an explicit parity test (including a snapshot confirming only DRAIN_SETS.adam changed); the 8 added kinds are additive-only (no removals), so dispatch.cjs's WARN check can only become MORE permissive (fewer false WARNs), never introduce a new WARN.

risks.risk: A naive repoint of FR-2/FR-3 that passes resolveRecognizedKinds() output straight into the generic inbox predicates would consume handler-owned kinds (ADAM_EXCLUDED_KINDS for Adam; comms_check for Solomon) that DRAIN_SETS deliberately includes for OTHER reasons (e.g. dispatch.cjs's WARN check), breaking their dedicated first-class handling paths -- caught during PLAN-phase TESTING sub-agent review before EXEC began.
risks.mitigation: FR-2/FR-3 now explicitly require subtracting the relevant exclusion set before generic-inbox consumption; TS-2/TS-3 add negative assertions that fail on a non-subtracting (naive) repoint.

risks.risk: Generalizing selectUnactionedAdvisories's kind filter directly (the original FR-4 scoping) would create a permanent phantom count for kinds whose retirement semantics differ from adam_advisory's actioned_at-based retirement -- caught during PLAN-phase TESTING sub-agent review.
risks.mitigation: FR-4 rescoped to generalize ONLY readSalientState's openSignalCount salience check (an OR-in, not a replacement), explicitly leaving selectUnactionedAdvisories untouched (TR-5, TS-5). Properly generalizing per-kind retirement-predicate semantics is flagged as an explicit follow-on, NOT this SD's job.

risks.risk: Removing ADAM_INBOX_KINDS/SOLOMON_INBOX_KINDS constants entirely (rather than leaving them as dead code) could break an unnoticed third caller.
risks.mitigation: Repo-wide grep (already run during PLAN research) confirms each constant has exactly one consumer (its own isXInboxRow predicate) before removal; FR-5's static guard also catches any future reintroduction.

risks.risk: FOLLOW-ON (out of scope for this SD): selectUnactionedAdvisories's single-kind + actioned_at-coupled retirement logic still does not generalize to other kinds a future sender might add to the coordinator's/adam's drain set.
risks.mitigation: Explicitly deferred; requires a per-kind retirement-predicate mapping design, which is larger than this SD's scope. Flag as a completion-flags finding at SD close so it is tracked, not silently dropped.

data_contracts: No new tables/columns. FR-1 only adds entries to the existing in-memory DRAIN_SETS.adam JS constant.
observability_rollout: Each repointed reader inherits the registry-reader's existing stderr canary (fires while role_drain_sets remains unapplied) -- no new observability surface, reusing Child A's.

system_architecture.summary: Three existing inbox-reader predicates swap a locally-owned kind-list constant for a resolved-kinds parameter sourced from Child A's registry-reader lib; one shared hard-coded constant (DRAIN_SETS.adam) is reconciled first so the swap is behavior-neutral.

components.name: scripts/adam-advisory.cjs
components.change: ADAM_INBOX_KINDS removed; isAdamInboxRow takes resolved kinds as a parameter (FR-2)

components.name: scripts/solomon-advisory.cjs
components.change: SOLOMON_INBOX_KINDS removed; isSolomonInboxRow takes resolved kinds as a parameter (FR-3)

components.name: lib/coordinator/adam-advisory-store.cjs / scripts/coordinator-quiet-tick.mjs
components.change: Single-kind hard-code generalized to a resolved-kinds check (FR-4)

components.name: tests/static-guards/drain-set-registry-readers.test.js
components.change: NEW -- static guard for residual hand-rolled kind-list constants (FR-5)

implementation_approach.summary: Reconcile the shared DRAIN_SETS.adam constant first (lowest-risk-if-done-carefully, highest-blast-radius-if-wrong), then repoint each of the 3 reader sites one at a time with a parity test before moving to the next, then the tick-lane generalization (the one genuinely new behavior, not just a refactor), then the static guard last (so it can verify the end state rather than a partial one).
steps: FR-1: reconcile DRAIN_SETS.adam, add TS-1 parity test
FR-2: repoint Adam inbox, add TS-2
FR-3: repoint Solomon drain, add TS-3
FR-4: generalize the coordinator tick lane's kind-check, add TS-4/TS-5
FR-5: add the static guard (TS-6), confirm TS-7 (hasUnactionedDirective untouched) still passes

smoke_test_steps.instruction: node -e "const {DRAIN_SETS}=require('./lib/fleet/worker-status.cjs'); console.log(DRAIN_SETS.adam.length)" before and after FR-1
smoke_test_steps.expected_outcome: Count increases by exactly 8 after FR-1, with no other role's array changed.

smoke_test_steps.instruction: Confirm scripts/adam-advisory.cjs and scripts/solomon-advisory.cjs no longer export/define ADAM_INBOX_KINDS/SOLOMON_INBOX_KINDS: grep -rn 'ADAM_INBOX_KINDS\|SOLOMON_INBOX_KINDS' scripts/
Run the new static guard directly: npx vitest run tests/static-guards/drain-set-registry-readers.test.js
smoke_test_steps.expected_outcome: All assertions pass against the fully-repointed tree.
